      Snowflake Confidential

      Prepared by Snowflake · This estimate is based on stated requirements and industry benchmarks. Actual consumption may vary. All prices are list price on-demand rates. Credit rates effective __PDF_VERSION__.

      All figures are expressed in US dollars (USD). No currency conversion is applied.

      Generated __DATE__

      How to use this page

      Action Buttons (top-right)

      • Export to PPTX : Generates and downloads a Snowflake-branded .pptx directly in the browser - no local server required.
      • Save HTML : Snapshots all current edits into a self-contained .html file and increments the version number. Open the saved file to continue editing.
      • Print / Save PDF : Opens the browser print dialog. Choose Save as PDF for a clean Snowflake-branded PDF; all editing controls are hidden in print layout.

      Configuration Tabs

      • Global Settings : Cloud/region, edition, credit rate, contract years, growth rate, discount override, and ramp-curve defaults.
      • Warehouses : Add and configure virtual warehouse workloads: size, hours/day, cluster count, warehouse type (Gen 1/2/Snowpark), and per-workload ramp schedule.
      • Serverless : Toggle features (Snowpipe, Tasks, Auto-clustering, Search Optimization, etc.) and enter monthly volumes.
      • AI / Cortex : Cortex Complete, Agents, Search, Analyst, Document AI, Cortex Code surfaces, and embeddings.
      • SPCS : Snowpark Container Services compute pools by instance family and hours.
      • OpenFlow : OpenFlow connector instances: warehouse MERGE hours and Snowpipe Streaming row volumes.
      • Storage : Raw TB, compression ratio, time-travel days, churn rate, and annual growth.
      • Collaboration : Reader and Managed Account compute sizing.
      • Replication : Cross-region replication: initial dataset, monthly change rate, source/target regions, and egress pricing.

      Editing the Sizing

      • Add workloads / instances : Use the “+ Add …” buttons at the bottom of each tab. Click a workload’s name to rename it inline.
      • Delete : Hover a workload, SPCS, or OpenFlow card to reveal the delete (×) button in its top-right corner.
      • Assumptions & Confirmations : Click any bullet to edit inline; hover to reveal the × delete button; click “+ Add” to append a new item.
      • Scenarios : The three scenario cards (Conservative / Expected / Aggressive) let you tune growth rate, ramp curve, and go-live shift. Toggle the checkbox above to show or hide the outer two.
      • ⓘ icons : Hover or focus any ⓘ icon next to a field for a short definition of that feature or metric.
